name: Med Link
recent_news: No M&A or school-contract news; appears to be a small non-emergency medical transport (NEMT) van operator in the Phoenix MSA. NPI registry lists Med-Link Transportation Inc as a non-emergency medical transport van provider.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Sub-scale NEMT van service (~8 employees, ~$600K rev) with no apparent school district contracts; outside Summit's K-12 yellow-bus tuck-in profile.

name: Specialty Transport
recent_news: BBB-listed Spokane-area oxygen/wheelchair-accessible transport provider; no M&A activity found. Spokane Public Schools' yellow-bus contract was awarded to Zum (replacing Durham) in 2023, with no involvement from Specialty Transport.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Small independent NEMT/wheelchair van operator, not a school-bus contractor. Too small (~7 employees, ~$500K rev) and wrong service line for Summit tuck-in.

name: Caregiver Assist Trnsprtn
service_types: non-medical/handicap transport for elderly and disabled
recent_news: Small Covington, GA non-emergency/handicap transport operator serving metro Atlanta; no acquisition or school-contract news found.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Senior/disabled non-medical NEMT in metro Atlanta; not a yellow-bus or SPED school contractor. Sub-scale and outside Summit's target profile.

name: Star Medicar Transportation
recent_news: FMCSA-registered (USDOT 1356661) small Chicago-area medical transport carrier; no M&A activity or school district contracts identified.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Tiny (~5 employees, ~$378K rev) Chicago NEMT operator; not a K-12 school-bus contractor and outside Summit's tuck-in profile.

name: P M Transportation
recent_news: Listed in USDOT DBE program (P & M Transportation, LLC); operates as a Fayetteville, GA medical/NEMT provider. No school-district contracts or acquisition activity identified.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Small DBE-certified medical/NEMT operator south of Atlanta; not a school-bus contractor. Sub-scale (~6 employees, ~$377K rev) and outside Summit's K-12 tuck-in scope.
